title: Hedeoma camporum
taxonomy: Hedeoma camporum is a species of flowering plant in the mint family, Lamiaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is a perennial herb that grows to about 10-30 inches in height. Hedeoma camporum is classified as a member of the genus Hedeoma, which includes several other species of mint plants. The specific classification of Hedeoma camporum is Hedeoma camporum (A. Gray) Benth.
uses: Hedeoma camporum, also known as Oregon mountain pennyroyal, is a plant with a rich history of medicinal uses. Traditionally, it was used by Native American communities to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's leaves and stems contain essential oils that possess anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which contribute to its medicinal value. In modern times, Hedeoma camporum is being studied for its potential to treat conditions such as anxiety and insomnia. However, further research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential applications.

intro: Hedeoma camporum, commonly known as woodland pennyroyal, is a perennial herb native to North America, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The plant is valued for its carminative, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are said to aid in digestion, relieve menstrual cramps, and combat infections. Bioactive constituents of Hedeoma camporum include volatile oils such as pulegone, menthone, and limonene, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids. Woodland pennyroyal is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which are used to support women's health, relieve respiratory issues, and promote overall well-being.
